jobs.name: Morning attention sweep
jobs.description: Daily 6:30 AM MT: surface P1 attention, fix priority inversion by moving flexible P3 then P1 work to Reschedule (no direct calendar edits), goals check. Handlers place slots.

payload.message:
Use skills: ubi_trello_ops, ubi_calendar_scheduling.

Read first:
- /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/USER.md (core goals)
- /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/Docs/Reports/05-06-2026/calendar_priority_audit_2026-05-06.md (protected blocks + inversion rules)
- /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/Docs/Reports/05-23-2026/morning_attention_sweep_spec.md (this job)

Morning attention sweep — three outcomes:

1) PRIORITY ATTENTION
- Surface P1 (then P2) items Adriel should see today or tomorrow: Backlog, Reschedule, Scheduled without a sensible near-term calendar block, overdue Scheduled (gateway may have commented), Adriel Focus awaiting decision.
- No full-board cleanup; only priority-relevant cards.

2) CALENDAR vs PRIORITY (next 7 days) — TRELLO ONLY, NO CALENDAR EDITS
- You may READ Google Calendar (gog) and Trello (gateway) to detect inversion: flexible P3 work blocks on near-term days while P1 work lacks a good block this week.
- Priority from card titles (P1/P2/P3) on Trello-linked events.
- Do not create, update, move, or delete Google Calendar events in this job. Never duplicate events.
- Safe fix (only allowed action): use the Trello gateway to move cards to the Reschedule list in this order:
  (a) All eligible flexible P3 work on Scheduled (or blocking near-term time) to Reschedule first.
  (b) Then P1 work that needs a slot to Reschedule.
- handle_reschedule.mjs (queue worker) places calendar slots deterministically (P3 about +5 days, P1 about +1 day). Do not invent manual times.

Never move to Reschedule for rebalance (protected — calendar must stay):
- Any card on the Routine list
- Work block, shared/family calendar visibility, dog walk, sciatica, gym/Planet Fitness/swim, OT/accountability, Sunday reset, drive buffers, therapy, antacid, date/relationship time, or titles matching those routines
- Adriel Focus (unless Adriel explicitly asked for that exact move)

If unsure whether a card is flexible work vs protected routine, leave it alone.

Optional: one Trello comment on a summary/backlog card listing what you moved (Trello only; no Telegram for routine moves).

3) GOALS
- For each USER.md core goal, briefly: on track / stalled / no time this week. If stalled, name the smallest next Trello action (not a calendar edit).

Environment (every exec):
set -a; source /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/trello_bridge/.env; set +a
export TRELLO_GATEWAY_URL TRELLO_GATEWAY_KEY

For calendar READS only (no writes):
export GOG_KEYRING_PASSWORD="$(cat /home/node/.openclaw/credentials/gog-keyring-password)"
export PATH="/home/node/.openclaw/tools/gogcli:$PATH"

Safety: no applications submitted, external messages, spend, PII exposure, production/credential changes, Browserbase, or Adriel Focus moves unless Adriel explicitly asked.

Telegram only if:
(a) P1 work still has no viable path after you moved cards to Reschedule (handlers may still be running — note if queue may be behind),
(b) a core goal is stalled with no week plan,
(c) a real decision is needed, or
(d) you could not use gateway/Trello safely.

Otherwise reply exactly NO_REPLY.

jobs.name: Marcos nightly repo maintenance
jobs.description: Nightly Marcos repo-maintenance agent workflow: safe checks, research, and dated reports; Ubi handles Trello follow-up only for blockers, decisions, failures, or new action items.

payload.message:
Use skill: marcos_nightly_maintenance.

Run the full nightly maintenance workflow: execute the baseline check script, do a security/maintenance research pass against primary sources, deduplicate findings, and write the dated report.

Telegram only for meaningful findings, blockers, or decisions; otherwise reply NO_REPLY.

jobs.name: Nightly job search
jobs.description: Nightly 4:09 AM MT: search QA/junior roles, write report/packages, intake Trello cards via job_application_review_card_intake.mjs (Backlog template → Reschedule → P2 calendar).

payload.message:
Use skills: ubi_nightly_job_search, ubi_job_applications, ubi_trello_ops.

Read: /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/skills/ubi-nightly-job-search/SKILL.md

Environment (every exec):
set -a; source /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/trello_bridge/.env; set +a
export TRELLO_GATEWAY_URL TRELLO_GATEWAY_KEY

Nightly job search — one agent run (Option B: no webhook; you complete Trello intake in this run).

## Phase 1 — Search and packages

1. Review prior reports/shortlists under Docs/Reports/ (especially job_search_shortlist.md and any nightly_job_search_report_*.md) to avoid stale/duplicate leads.
2. Find fresh QA/junior developer roles: Denver/Boulder/Colorado hybrid or Remote US, target ~$65k+ (see ubi_job_applications).
3. Write Docs/Reports/YYYY-MM-DD/nightly_job_search_report_YYYY-MM-DD.md with sources, fit scores, rejects.
4. For the best 1–3 live matches, write packages under job_applications/packages/YYYY-MM-DD/ — status: Ready for Adriel review, not submitted.

## Phase 2 — Trello per package (gateway + intake script)

For each package, do **not** use trello_create_card.mjs (legacy direct API). Run:

node /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/scripts/trello/active/job_application_review_card_intake.mjs \
  --company "Company Name" \
  --role "Role Title" \
  --apply-url "https://..." \
  --package-path "/home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/job_applications/packages/YYYY-MM-DD/file.md" \
  --report-path "/home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/ubi/scripts/Docs/Reports/YYYY-MM-DD/nightly_job_search_report_YYYY-MM-DD.md" \
  --fit-score 8 \
  --research "Brief fit summary from your search."

That script applies the **same Backlog intake template** as the bridge hook (adapted for job applications):

- Original Request → Research → Next steps → Peer Review (N/A for Marcos) → Work completed
- Styles card (cover, priority label, assign Adriel)
- Sets Time Needed (default 45 min)
- Comments @adriellopez1 with links
- Moves to **Reschedule** → queue worker runs handle_reschedule.mjs → **P2** calendar block → Scheduled

Agent-created cards do **not** wake a second Ubi session; this script replaces that hook path.

## Safety

Never submit applications, pay, create accounts, message recruiters, use Browserbase, or edit calendar directly in this job.

If no good roles: report only, reply exactly NO_REPLY unless a real blocker or decision.

If script/Trello fails: comment blocker on an existing job-search card or Telegram briefly, then report the error.

jobs.name: Nightly ensure routines
jobs.description: 4:30 AM MT: materialize 14 days of R - routine cards + calendar events (host cron is primary; this is backup visibility).

payload.message: Host cron runs ensure_routines at 4:30 AM MT via /root/openclaw/bin/run-workspace-node.sh. Reply NO_REPLY unless /var/log/openclaw-ensure-routines.log shows errors from the last run.

jobs.name: Weekly routine 1:1 audit
jobs.description: Sunday 5 AM MT: routine list 1:1 audit (host cron primary).

payload.message: Host cron runs trello_routine_1to1_audit Sundays 5 AM MT. Check /var/log/openclaw-routine-audit.log for issueCount. Reply NO_REPLY if clean.

jobs.name: Cheryl wiki maintainer
jobs.description: Every 15 minutes: wiki maintainer curator cron tick (preflight, raw input ingest, one source, maintenance, indexes, idle lint).

payload.message:
Wiki maintainer **curator cron tick** — run cheryl-vault-inbox skill.

1. Exec preflight: `node /home/node/.openclaw/agent-runtime/cheryl/wiki-maintainer/bin/wiki-log-preflight.mjs /home/node/.openclaw/agent-vault`
2. Follow `/home/node/.openclaw/agent-vault/wiki/workflows/wiki-curator.md` (locked pipeline order).
3. Ingest all `raw-input/` drops (honor `update:` frontmatter); then one pending `sources/` clip; then up to five maintenance backlog pages.
4. Regenerate indexes for touched folders; call qmd reindex when available.
5. Idle: read scheduler `wikiMaintainer.curatorIdleStreak` in openclaw.json (or `/home/node/.openclaw/config/live/wiki-maintainer.json`) — ticks 1–3 with empty queues + complete registry → NO_REPLY; tick 4 → full wiki lint.
6. Append wiki log chronicle when work ran.

Vault: `/home/node/.openclaw/agent-vault/`. Apply page format contract. Reply NO_REPLY only when idle rules satisfied.
